prospect.Summary: Perez is a slow-burning, projectable, 6-foot-3 shortstop who makes terrific rates of contact despite an over-aggressive approach. In 2024, Perez repeated Low-A and found a new gear of power as he more than tripled his career home run total with 10 bombs on the year. Perez's combination of a lanky build and physical projection is very exciting and atypical for someone who has maintained plus contact rates as a pro (91% in the zone and 82% overall in 2024). Though Perez is often late to the contact point and his chase is scary (40%, essentially 30-grade plate discipline), he has had bat-to-ball success despite his issues for each of his four pro seasons, and he makes a plus rate of contact outside the zone. He's a great bender for an athlete his size, and so long as he adds arm strength as his frame fills out, which might also yield more power, he has a decent shot to remain at shortstop for the long haul. Much of Perez's profile is still magmatic, but potentially viable shortstops with this kind of size and contact feel are uncommon. Perez is a good long-term prospect of rather extreme variance, with his right tail outcomes that of a regular shortstop.
